svg.text: TAMIL
HISTORY & DRAVIDIAN CIVILIZATIONS
TAMIL ORIGINS
~300 BCE: Tamil-Brahmi inscriptions — oldest Dravidian writing
Sangam literature (300 BCE-300 CE): world-class ancient poetry
Sangam = "assembly of poets" at Madurai — 8 anthologies survive
Tolkappiyam (~1st c. BCE): grammar text; first comprehensive Indian grammar
Chola, Pandya, Chera kingdoms: three ancient Tamil dynasties
TAMIL EMPIRES
Pallava dynasty (275-897 CE): Tamil cultural peak; spread to SE Asia
Chola Empire (848-1279 CE): naval power; temples across India and SE Asia
Thiruvalluvar: Kural (1-2 CE?): 1330 couplets on life, ethics, love
Kural translated into 80+ languages — Tamil philosophic peak
TAMIL TODAY
~80M speakers; official in Tamil Nadu, Sri Lanka, Singapore, Malaysia
One of 22 scheduled languages of India; classical language status
TELUGU HISTORY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
TELUGU ORIGINS
~400-500 CE: earliest Telugu inscriptions (later than Tamil)
Proto-Dravidian: common ancestor of Tamil and Telugu ~3000 BCE
Telugu and Tamil diverged earlier than Romance languages diverged
TELUGU LITERARY GOLDEN AGE
Nannaya (11th c.): translated Mahabharata into Telugu — first great work
Tikkana (13th c.) + Errana (14th c.): completed Mahabharata translation
Vijayanagara Empire (14th-17th c.): Telugu language and culture peak
Tenali Ramakrishna: court poet; "Telugu Birbal" for wit and wisdom
TELUGU TODAY
~85M speakers; largest Dravidian language by speakers
Official: Andhra Pradesh + Telangana (two separate states since 2014)
Telugu film (Tollywood): 2nd largest film industry in India
DRAVIDIAN CIVILIZATIONS
Tamil and Telugu: two of four classical Dravidian languages
Indus Valley (~3300 BCE): possibly Dravidian (not yet deciphered)
Both languages: resisted Sanskrit dominance while absorbing it
Both: built temple civilizations across South and SE Asia
Two great Dravidian river civilizations: Kaveri (Tamil) • Krishna (Telugu)
PLATE 14d ALT · TAMIL / TELUGU · HISTORY
